scenario: Atlas Telco posts 6000 events per day in Camunda 8.8 and a developer needs a FEEL expression to detect whether today is a weekend so a customer-care subprocess can skip non-business days inside an exclusive gateway condition on the escalation routing flow.
question: Which FEEL expression correctly checks whether the current day falls on a weekend, according to the temporal examples page?

options.text: day of week(today()) in ("Saturday","Sunday") returns true when the weekday name matches one of the two weekend labels at evaluation time
weekday(today()) in (6,7) returns true since FEEL numbers Saturday and Sunday as 6 and 7 in the same expression style as JavaScript Date
today().weekend returns true on Saturday and Sunday using a boolean weekend property exposed by the date value at evaluation time inside FEEL
is weekend(today()) returns true on Saturday and Sunday using a dedicated FEEL helper provided by the temporal library inside Camunda 8

a.text: Correct. The Check for weekend example uses day of week(today()) in ("Saturday","Sunday") to detect a weekend day directly from the weekday name returned by the function.

b.text: Incorrect. The documented example uses the day of week function and string day names rather than the weekday property and numbers; that alternative is not what the page shows.

c.text: Incorrect. FEEL exposes properties such as weekday, year and month on dates, but there is no boolean weekend property in the documented properties table for date values.

d.text: Incorrect. There is no is weekend built-in function shown in the FEEL temporal examples; the documented pattern is day of week(today()) tested against the weekend names.

explanation: The Check for weekend example shows day of week(today()) in ("Saturday","Sunday") as the FEEL expression for detecting a weekend, combining the day of week function (which returns a weekday name) with a list membership test against the two weekend labels.
